pitch.title: Repo Mind
pitch.logline: When a corporate memory-repo agent repossesses a defaulting client's memories, he uncovers a hidden file depicting his own unsolved murder in a past life, forcing him to go rogue to find his killer before his current mind is wiped.
pitch.core_theme: The commodification of the human soul and the inescapable nature of the past.
pitch.synopsis:
Act I: In a dystopian future where memories are stored on expensive corporate servers, Elias works as a 'Repo Man,' legally extracting memories from defaulting citizens. He is cold, efficient, and unquestioning. During a routine extraction from an elderly woman, he accidentally syncs with a hidden, encrypted memory file. He experiences a vivid first-person memory of being murdered—and realizes the victim is a past version of himself.

Act II: Elias investigates the memory, discovering that the corporation he works for has been recycling consciousnesses for decades, wiping memories of those who get too close to their dark secrets. Hunted by his own ruthless colleagues and a specialized team of corporate assassins, Elias teams up with an underground network of 'memory pirates.' Together, they attempt to hack the central server to retrieve the rest of Elias's past life, leading to a series of mind-bending heists where the laws of physics are dictated by the memories they infiltrate.

Act III: Elias successfully breaches the corporate mainframe, only to face the CEO—the very man who murdered him in his past life. In a climactic battle fought simultaneously in the physical world and a collapsing digital memory-scape, Elias manages to broadcast the corporation's crimes to the world. He sacrifices his own digital immortality to destroy the servers, freeing humanity's memories and finally finding peace.

beats.description: Elias coldly extracts a cherished memory from a defaulting citizen, establishing his ruthless efficiency and the bleak status quo.
During a routine extraction from an elderly woman, Elias accidentally syncs with an encrypted hidden file and experiences a vivid first-person memory of being murdered by a shadowy figure.
Elias secretly analyzes the memory file, discovering that the victim is a past version of himself and realizing the corporation recycles consciousnesses.
Corporate monitors detect Elias's unauthorized access, prompting an assassination squad to attack his apartment, forcing him to abandon his life and flee into the city's underbelly.
Hunted and out of options, Elias seeks out an underground network of memory pirates who operate outside corporate control.
Elias learns to navigate the pirates' illegal memory-hacking tech, experiencing surreal digital landscapes where the laws of physics are dictated by the host's subconscious.
Elias and the pirates orchestrate a mind-bending heist into a mid-level corporate server to retrieve the missing fragments of his past life.
They successfully extract the data, revealing that the current corporate CEO is Elias's killer, but their breach triggers a massive system alarm that exposes their location.
The CEO dispatches a specialized team of corporate assassins who relentlessly track Elias's digital and physical footprints.
The assassins ambush the pirates' physical hideout while they are jacked into the network, resulting in a chaotic battle across two realities.
The pirates suffer heavy casualties, and the corporation initiates a remote wipe of Elias's current mind, causing his identity to disintegrate.
With his memories fading fast, Elias realizes he cannot run and decides on a suicidal mission to physically breach the central corporate mainframe.
Elias and the surviving pirates launch a desperate, two-pronged assault on the heavily fortified corporate headquarters.
Elias breaks into the server room and connects to the mainframe, facing the CEO in a climactic battle fought simultaneously in the physical world and a collapsing digital memory-scape.
Elias overpowers the CEO's digital avatar and manages to broadcast the corporation's dark secrets and recycled consciousness files to the entire world.
To finalize the broadcast, Elias destroys the primary servers, sacrificing his own digital immortality to free humanity's memories and finally finding peace as his mind fades.

characters.name: Elias
characters.brief_summary: A cold, ruthlessly efficient corporate memory-repo agent who extracts defaulting clients' memories. His rigid worldview shatters when he discovers a hidden memory of his own past-life murder, forcing him to rebel against the system he serves.

psychological_core.wound: The subconscious trauma of being murdered and having his identity repeatedly erased and recycled by the corporation.
psychological_core.false_belief: If I do my job perfectly and follow the rules, I am safe and my mind is my own.
psychological_core.fear: Complete erasure; losing his sense of self and becoming nothing.
psychological_core.desire: To find his killer and uncover the truth of his past life before his mind is wiped.
psychological_core.need: To accept that true humanity and peace come from sacrifice and letting go, not from hoarding memories or achieving digital immortality.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Clinical, precise, and economical. He uses corporate jargon like extraction, default, and sync even in casual settings. He NEVER uses nostalgic or emotional words like cherish or remember.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Citing corporate policy, statistics, or logistical facts when confronted with an emotional or moral question.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He rarely raises his voice or shows outright panic. His anxiety manifests in micro-adjustments, like straightening his cuffs or checking his sync-port. He only breaks his clinical tone when his own memories start glitching.

characters.name: Julian Vance
characters.brief_summary: The visionary, sociopathic CEO of the memory storage corporation who murdered Elias in a past life. He views human consciousness as a raw resource to be managed, believing he is the shepherd of human immortality.

psychological_core.wound: A deep-seated terror of mortality and being forgotten, stemming from an early, profound loss he could not control.
psychological_core.false_belief: Human beings are too fragile to handle their own existence; only corporate control can grant true immortality.
psychological_core.fear: Death, irrelevance, and the chaotic unpredictability of human free will.
psychological_core.desire: To maintain absolute control over the city's consciousness and ensure his own eternal digital dominion.
psychological_core.need: To realize that a controlled, stagnant eternity is a prison worse than death.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Warm, paternal, and philosophical. He speaks like a benevolent tech-guru or a therapist. He NEVER raises his voice in anger or uses crude language.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Reframing a victim's suffering as a necessary step in their personal evolution or changing the subject to the grand design of the future.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He projects absolute calm and empathy, acting as if he is doing his victims a favor. His sociopathy only peeks through when someone damages his servers, causing a momentary, terrifying stillness before he resumes his warm facade.

characters.name: Jax
characters.brief_summary: A cynical, tech-savvy memory pirate who lives off the grid and hacks corporate servers for thrills and profit. She becomes Elias's reluctant guide through the digital underworld and his moral anchor.

psychological_core.wound: Lost her original memories in a corporate wipe when her family defaulted on their storage payments.
psychological_core.false_belief: The past is just data; it does not matter who you were, only what you can steal today.
psychological_core.fear: Being trapped, categorized, or controlled by the corporation ever again.
psychological_core.desire: To pull off the ultimate heist and burn the corporate servers to the ground for a massive payout.
psychological_core.need: To fight for a cause bigger than herself and reclaim her own lost humanity and capacity to care.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Fast, sarcastic, and littered with hacker slang. She uses nicknames to keep people at a distance. She NEVER talks about her own past or uses the word home.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Using dark humor, sarcasm, or suddenly focusing intensely on her tech gear to avoid answering personal questions.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: She acts tough and detached, treating memory heists like video games. Her underlying pain only shows when she sees families being repoed; she will instinctively look away or grind her jaw, masking it immediately with a cynical joke.

characters.name: Martha
characters.brief_summary: A frail but defiant defaulting citizen whose memory extraction triggers Elias's awakening. She serves as the emotional catalyst that shatters Elias's corporate illusion.

psychological_core.wound: Outlived her loved ones and can no longer afford the subscription to keep their digital memories alive.
psychological_core.false_belief: My worth and identity are entirely tied to the digital records of the people I have lost.
psychological_core.fear: Forgetting the face and voice of her late husband.
psychological_core.desire: To hold onto her final, most precious memory at all costs.
psychological_core.need: To accept that love persists in the soul even when the digital record is gone.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Slow, reflective, and poetic. She speaks in sensory details like smells, sounds, and colors. She NEVER begs or uses corporate terminology.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Retreating into a fond, unrelated story from her youth when threatened by the repo process.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: She does not scream or fight physically during the repo. Her resistance is quiet and dignified, shown through a tight grip on her chair and a steady, accusatory stare that pierces Elias's professional armor.
